original_abstract:
This study examines the integration of philosophical ethics and prophetic values within Islamic education to foster a learning environment that is humanistic, equitable, and transformative. While philosophical ethics are grounded in human rationality and socio-cultural logic, prophetic values are derived from the divine revelations of the Qur’an and Hadith. This research employs a qualitative methodology, utilizing a literature study and content analysis to explore how synthesizing these two frameworks can enhance character development and social consciousness. 

The findings indicate that while their origins differ—one being rational and the other revelatory—both philosophical ethics and prophetic values converge on the principles of goodness, truth, and moral integrity. By bridging these perspectives, Islamic education can move beyond mere knowledge transfer to develop the "whole human," honoring inherent dignity (humanism), ensuring equal treatment and access (justice), and empowering students to act as catalysts for social progress (transformation). Ultimately, this integrated approach aims to cultivate a generation that balances academic excellence with spiritual intelligence and noble conduct, aligning modern educational needs with foundational Islamic principles.
humanized_abstract:
The relationship between philosophical ethics and prophetic values in Islamic education with a humanistic, balanced and advanced objective, is being studied in this research. Philosophical ethics as a branch of knowledge, are derived from the human logic and social civilization, while the prophetic values are derived from the Islamic text based on the verses of the Holy Qur’an and Hadith. Therefore, in this research, using qualitative method based on literature study and content analysis, the purpose is to investigate the effectiveness of combining these two sources with each other to the extent that it leads to the improvement of moral values and social awareness among Muslim learners.

They investigate how both rationalistic and revelatory schools of philosophy arrive at similar principles of ethics, specifically goodness, truth and justice and how upon this basis, the Islamic educational system could provide students with a comprehensive learning experience as a total human development experience, based on the principles of humanism which respects and develops human dignity, justice, which assures equal opportunities for all and transformation which makes the learner a productive agent for social change. All these dimensions are in line with contemporary educational demands, while at the same time serving the requirements of the Islamic Educational System which seeks to establish a learning environment where students can obtain high academic achievement in combination with spiritual polish and high moral standards.
